# MxlWeb

> MxlWeb is a browser-side ODE model explorer for systems biology. All compute runs client-side in web workers — no server required. Built with SvelteKit 5, adapter-static, and deployed at /mxl-web. Provides interactive equation editing, parameter scanning, and real-time visualization for kinetic and ODE models. Maintained by the Computational Biology group at RWTH Aachen.

Targets modern browsers (Chrome, Firefox, Safari, Edge). Part of a tool family with [MxlPy](https://github.com/Computational-Biology-Aachen/mxl-py) (Python mechanistic learning), [MxlBricks](https://github.com/Computational-Biology-Aachen/mxl-bricks) (composable reaction bricks), and [MxlWeb Core](https://github.com/Computational-Biology-Aachen/mxl-web-core) (shared compute engine).

## Architecture

```
Route page (src/routes/models/<name>/+page.svelte)
  → AnalysesDashboard (main simulation UI)
    → ModelEditor (equation / stoichiometry / parameter tables)
      → WorkerManager (jsWorker / pyWorker)
        → ODE integrators (Euler, RK45, BOSH3, Tsit5, Radau5, ...)
          → Chart.js visualization
```

- **Route pages** define `Model` and `Analyses` configs for each model.
- **AnalysesDashboard** is the main UI shell: simulation controls, result tabs, parameter sweeps.
- **ModelEditor** lets users edit variables, parameters, reactions, and derived quantities inline.
- **WorkerManager** dispatches computation to web workers (JS or Pyodide).
- **Core engine** lives in `@computational-biology-aachen/mxlweb-core`: model builders, IR, MathML→WAT pipeline, and all integrators.

## Available models

Each model has its own route at `/mxl-web/models/<name>/`:

- `lotka-volterra` — Classic predator-prey
- `sir` — SIR epidemiology model
- `poolman2000` — Cyanobacterial photosynthesis
- `yokota1985` — C4 photosynthesis
- `ebenhoeh2014` — Photosynthetic electron transport
- `matuszynska2016_npq` — Non-photochemical quenching
- `matuszynska2016_phd` — Photosynthetic electron transport (PhD model)
- `matuszynska2019` — Expanded photosynthesis model
- `saadat2021` — Photosynthesis with NPQ
- `ebeling-2026` — Electron transport chain
- `hahn1987` — Rubisco kinetics
- `lazar1997` — Photosynthetic regulation
- `davis2017` — Photosynthetic electron transport
- `bellasio2019` — Stomatal conductance
- `fuente2024` — Leaf-level photosynthesis
- `li2021` — Photosynthetic electron transport
- `zhu2009` — Photoinhibition
- `fvcb` — FvCB photosynthesis model
- `kea3-tomato` — KEA3 transport in tomato
- `population-dynamics` — Population dynamics
- `tripartite` — Tripartite interaction
- `dynamic-entrobactin` — Entrobactin dynamics

## Compute backends

Three compute backends run in web workers:

- **Pure JS** — Euler, RK2, RK45, BOSH3, Tsit5, backward Euler, Kvaerno45
- **Pyodide** — Full Python/WASM runtime for arbitrary model code
- **Custom WASM** — Radau5, DOP853, DOPRI5 (C compiled via Emscripten)

The default backend is pure JS. WASM integrators offer stiff-solver support. Pyodide enables running Python-defined models directly in the browser.

## Quick start

> Clone and run locally:

```bash
git clone https://github.com/Computational-Biology-Aachen/mxl-web.git
cd mxl-web
npm install
npm run dev
```

Open http://localhost:5173/models/lotka-volterra to run the Lotka-Volterra model interactively.

> For programmatic use, import from `@computational-biology-aachen/mxlweb-core`:

```typescript
import { KineticModelBuilder } from '@computational-biology-aachen/mxlweb-core'
import { simulate } from '@computational-biology-aachen/mxlweb-core/backends/js'

const builder = new KineticModelBuilder()
builder
  .addVariable('S', 1.0)
  .addVariable('P', 0.0)
  .addParameter('k1', 0.5)
  .addReaction('v1', 'k1 * S', { S: -1, P: 1 })

const ir = builder.toIR()
const result = await simulate(ir, { tEnd: 10, nSteps: 100 })
```

## Dev commands

```bash
npm install              # Install dependencies
npm run dev              # Dev server on :5173
npm run build            # Static build → build/
npm run check            # TypeScript + Svelte type checking
npm run lint             # Prettier + ESLint check
npm run format           # Auto-format with Prettier
npm run test             # Run vitest
```

## MCP tool reference

For AI-assisted modeling, see the [mxl-mcp](https://computational-biology-aachen.github.io/mxl-mcp/mxlpy/latest) documentation.

## Tool family

- [MxlPy](https://github.com/Computational-Biology-Aachen/mxl-py) — Python mechanistic learning (ODEs + ML)
- [MxlBricks](https://github.com/Computational-Biology-Aachen/mxl-bricks) — Composable reaction building blocks
- [MxlModels](https://github.com/Computational-Biology-Aachen/mxl-models) — Flat single-file model versions (generated from MxlBricks)
- [Mxl-Schemas](https://github.com/Computational-Biology-Aachen/mxl-schemas) — JSON schemas for model formats
- [MxlWeb Core](https://github.com/Computational-Biology-Aachen/mxl-web-core) — Shared compute engine (TypeScript)
- [pysbml](https://github.com/Computational-Biology-Aachen/pysbml) — SBML import/export bridge
- [parameteriser](https://github.com/Computational-Biology-Aachen/parameteriser) — Kinetic parameter lookup (BRENDA DB)
